title: SQL Code Editor with Syntax Highlighting and Filtering
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to view my data mapping SQL output in a proper code editor with syntax highlighting and inline comments, so that I can easily read, review, and share the transformation logic with my development team.
description:
Replace the GPT-style typing animation with a proper SQL code editor using Prism.js for syntax highlighting. The rendered SQL should incorporate:

- **Migration status filtering**: Fields flagged as "do not migrate" are excluded from the SQL output
- **Notes and logic as comments**: The Notes and Logic fields from the migration table are rendered as SQL comments before each field
- **Field mapping inclusion**: The source-to-target mapping is shown inline (e.g., `SimpleLead.First Name as FirstName`)
- **System-generated header**: A comment indicating the code is system-generated as a guide
- **Developer notes field**: A new field on the object to include as a comment block

The output uses Prism.js for SQL syntax highlighting, replacing the previous GPT-style typewriter rendering.
acceptance_criteria: SQL output is rendered with Prism.js syntax highlighting
Fields marked 'do not migrate' are excluded from the output
Notes field content appears as SQL comments before the corresponding field
Logic field content appears as SQL comments before the corresponding field
Source-to-target mapping is shown as 'SourceObject.SourceField AS TargetField'
System-generated header comment is included at the top
ux_changes: SQL output modal replaced with Prism.js-powered code editor with syntax highlighting. GPT-style typewriter animation removed. Notes and logic fields rendered as inline SQL comments.
middleware_changes: SQL generation logic updated to include notes/logic as comments, filter out 'do not migrate' fields, and produce the new format with mapping details.
api_changes: SQL generation endpoint updated to include notes, logic, and migration status filtering in the output.
implementation_notes: Prism.js library integrated for SQL syntax highlighting. The 'do not migrate' filter was implemented first, followed by the comments integration. There was confusion about whether to also exclude 'Not Reviewed' status fields — this was flagged for clarification. The code was deployed to both staging and production.
comment_summary: Developer needed clarification on the 'do not migrate' flag location and the task scope. The filter for 'do not migrate' was implemented, then Prism.js styling was added. A re-test showed some fields were only rendering field names without mappings. Developer asked whether 'Not Reviewed' status should also be excluded.
